title: ctenopteris allocota
intro: Ctenopteris allocota, also known as the New Zealand fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Ctenopteris allocota include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to soothe skin irritations, calm respiratory issues, and promote overall well-being.
